name: Juan Domingo Perón
role: President 1946–1955, 1973–1974; Founder of Peronism
quote: The only truth is reality.

name: Eva 'Evita' Perón
role: First Lady 1946–1952; Champion of women's suffrage and social welfare
quote: I will come back, and I will be millions.

name: José de San Martín
role: General and Liberator; Commander of Army of the Andes, 1817
quote: I am tired of being called a liberator and not acting as one; the true liberator of the people is the people themselves.

name: Jorge Rafael Videla
role: Military dictator 1976–1981; Leader of Dirty War junta
quote: A terrorist is not just someone with a gun or bomb, but also someone who spreads ideas that are contrary to Western Christian civilization.

name: Javier Milei
role: President 2023–present; Libertarian economist and political outsider
quote: The State is not the solution. The State is the problem.

name: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner
role: President 2007–2015; Vice President 2019–2023; Kirchnerist leader
quote: I don't govern for the market. I govern for the Argentine people.

name: Néstor Kirchner
role: President 2003–2007; Led post-2001 recovery
quote: Argentina does not have a problem of governance. What we have is a problem of justice.

name: Raúl Alfonsín
role: President 1983–1989; Restored democracy; Juicio a las Juntas
quote: With democracy, one eats, one is educated, one is cured.

name: Carlos Menem
role: President 1989–1999; Convertibility Plan; mass privatizations
quote: Whoever touches the peso touches the sun.

name: Mauricio Macri
role: President 2015–2019; PRO party founder; center-right reformist
quote: I believe in gradual change, not shock therapy.

name: Domingo Faustino Sarmiento
role: President 1868–1874; Father of Argentine public education
quote: To govern is to educate.

name: Hipólito Yrigoyen
role: President 1916–1922, 1928–1930; First popular president, UCR founder
quote: The Radical Party is not a party; it is the Argentine cause.

name: Julio Argentino Roca
role: President 1880–1886, 1898–1904; Led Conquest of the Desert
quote: Paz y Administración — Peace and Administration.

name: Domingo Cavallo
role: Economy Minister 1991–1996, 2001; Architect of Convertibility Plan
quote: One peso, one dollar. It is the law.

name: Isabel Perón (María Estela Martínez)
role: President 1974–1976; First woman head of state in the world via succession
quote: I only obeyed orders that came from my husband's political will.

name: Leopoldo Galtieri
role: Military dictator 1981–1982; Ordered Malvinas invasion; convicted war criminal
quote: We have recovered the Malvinas. They have always been ours and will always be ours.

name: Victoria Villarruel
role: Vice President 2023–present; Defense policy expert; military family background
quote: The victims of terrorism deserve the same justice as all other victims.

name: Manuel Belgrano
role: General and statesman; Creator of the Argentine national flag (1812)
quote: With the flag that I have the honor of presenting, the nation must either win or die.

name: Alberto Nisman
role: AMIA Special Prosecutor 2004–2015; accused Iran of AMIA bombing; found dead Jan 2015
quote: I may be wrong. But if I am right and no one does anything, the consequences will be terrible.

name: Arturo Frondizi
role: President 1958–1962; Desarrollismo; overthrown by military coup
quote: Petroleum is our destiny. Without energy, Argentina cannot develop.

name: Juan Manuel de Rosas
role: Governor of Buenos Aires 1829–1832 and 1835–1852; Federalist caudillo; de facto ruler of the Argentine Confederation
quote: When the country is in danger, everything is permissible except not defending it.

name: Bartolomé Mitre
role: First President of unified Argentina 1862–1868; General, historian, and founder of La Nación newspaper (1870)
quote: The Argentine Republic is not made up of one city; it is made up of provinces that belong to all Argentines.

name: Alberto Fernández
role: President 2019–2023; Frente de Todos coalition; inherited debt crisis, governed through COVID-19 pandemic with 100%+ annual inflation at exit
quote: We did not achieve our goals — like strengthening income, fighting inflation, and reducing poverty.

name: Estela de Carlotto
role: President of Abuelas de Plaza de Mayo since 1989; her daughter Laura was killed by the junta; her grandson Ignacio was recovered in 2014 after a 36-year search
quote: The grandmothers have no hate or vengeance. We have love to help. We want justice under the law. Our motto: Memory, truth, and justice.

name: Eduardo Duhalde
role: Transitional President January 2002 – May 2003; devalued and floated the peso, ending Convertibility and enabling Argentina's economic recovery
quote: The only way out of this catastrophe is through production and work.

name: Hebe de Bonafini
role: Co-founder and President of Madres de Plaza de Mayo (1979–2022); her son Jorge was disappeared in 1976; icon of resistance to the Dirty War
quote: I do not want you to understand our pain; I want you to understand our struggle.

name: Rodolfo Walsh
role: Investigative journalist and writer; author of 'Operación Masacre' (1957); mailed his 'Open Letter to the Military Junta' on March 24, 1977 and was disappeared the following day by ESMA operatives
quote: The planned misery of millions of human beings cannot be explained by mere repression — it is the deliberate result of economic policy.

name: Fernando de la Rúa
role: President 1999–2001 (UCR/La Alianza); resigned December 20, 2001 by helicopter from the Casa Rosada roof as protesters occupied Plaza de Mayo; 39 civilians killed that day
quote: I leave a country in crisis to those who believe they can govern it better.

name: Ernesto 'Che' Guevara
role: Revolutionary leader and physician; born Rosario, June 14, 1928; architect of Cuban Revolution alongside Fidel Castro; executed by CIA-backed Bolivian forces on October 9, 1967
quote: Be realistic, demand the impossible.
